text:
It was a sunny Saturday morning. I decided to hop on my bike and explore some park trails. The air was fresh, and birds were singing happily.

After a while, I noticed a peculiar sight. Up ahead, a group of pigeons was playing. One dove onto the branches of a tree, bounced about, then took off excitedly. Another dove after it, joining in the fun.

I chuckled at their playful antics. Just then, a big shadow loomed over them. A massive owl swooped gracefully, its sharp eyes watching carefully.

The pigeons scattered, fluttering away. I couldn't help but laugh. That was one unusual birdwatching experience.

As the sun climbed higher, I found a bench to sit on and surveyed the park. A girl with a dog passed by, chasing a squirrel. She laughed heartily.

Just then, a little rabbit hoppered toward me. I bent down, making my profile less obvious. The rabbit seemed curious but quickly turned away
